reasoning: Without access to external search tools, the estimate is based on historical GISTEMP data and climate trends. October anomalies historically stayed below 1.10°C prior to the 2023 strong El Niño (e.g., Oct 2022 was 0.97°C, Oct 2021 was 0.99°C). While 2023 was exceptionally high (1.34°C), the ENSO cycle typically transitions to La Niña in the years following a strong El Niño, which often depresses temperatures globally. Thus, late 2025 is likely to experience La Niña or neutral conditions. However, factoring in long-term warming trends (approx +0.2-0.3°C per decade) and possible step-increases in base warming from reduced aerosol emissions, a La Niña or neutral October 2025 could hover very close to the 1.10°C threshold (around 1.05°C - 1.15°C). This makes the outcome highly uncertain and right on the border, giving approximately even odds.
